rawText:
When a disaster strikes — a flood, an earthquake, or a large fire — one of the first things emergency teams need is an accurate, up-to-date map of the affected area.

Without reliable maps, rescue workers cannot identify which roads are still passable, where shelters have been set up, or how many people live in the zones most at risk.

In many parts of the world, detailed maps simply do not exist, because the areas were never formally surveyed or because the data is locked behind expensive licenses.

This is where public mapping projects have made an enormous difference.

Platforms like OpenStreetMap allow volunteers around the world to add roads, buildings, and landmarks to a shared digital map that anyone can use for free.

During the 2010 earthquake in Haiti, thousands of online volunteers traced satellite images and created a usable street map of Port-au-Prince within days, giving aid organizations a tool that had not existed before the disaster.

Since then, the same approach has been repeated in dozens of emergencies, from typhoons in the Philippines to flooding in Mozambique.

The maps are designed to show not just geography but also critical information: the locations of hospitals, water sources, evacuation routes, and areas where buildings have collapsed.

Because the data is open, any relief agency can download it, print it, or load it onto a phone without waiting for permission or paying a fee.

Speed matters in emergencies — every hour spent searching for basic spatial information is an hour that could have been used to deliver supplies or evacuate residents.

Public maps also help communities prepare before a disaster happens, because residents can see which neighborhoods lie in flood zones and where the nearest safe areas are located.

Governments that support open mapping initiatives invest relatively little money but gain a tool that can save lives when it is needed most.

The value of a good map is easy to overlook during ordinary times, yet it becomes instantly obvious the moment a crisis begins.

Sharing geographic knowledge freely is not just a technical convenience — it is a matter of public safety.

overview.title: Why Public Maps Matter in Emergencies

sentences.text: When something bad happens like a flood or earthquake, helpers need good maps.
sentences.translation: 홍수나 지진 같은 나쁜 일이 생기면, 도와주는 사람들에게 좋은 지도가 필요해요.

sentences.text: But many places in the world do not have maps at all.
sentences.translation: 하지만 세계의 많은 곳에는 지도가 아예 없어요.

sentences.text: So people on the internet work together to make free maps that anyone can use.
sentences.translation: 그래서 인터넷에 있는 사람들이 함께 모여 누구나 쓸 수 있는 무료 지도를 만들어요.

sentences.text: These maps show important things like hospitals and safe places.
sentences.translation: 이 지도는 병원이나 안전한 곳 같은 중요한 것들을 보여 줘요.

sentences.text: Maps can also help people get ready before a disaster comes.
sentences.translation: 지도는 재해가 오기 전에 사람들이 준비하는 데도 도움이 돼요.

sentences.text: Sharing maps for free can save lives, so it is very important.
sentences.translation: 지도를 무료로 나누면 생명을 구할 수 있어서, 아주 중요한 일이에요.

sentences.text: The article explains why accurate maps are essential during emergencies like floods, earthquakes, and fires.
sentences.translation: 이 글은 홍수, 지진, 화재 같은 비상 상황에서 정확한 지도가 왜 필수적인지 설명해요.

sentences.text: Many regions lack detailed maps because they were never surveyed or the data is too expensive to access.
sentences.translation: 많은 지역은 측량된 적이 없거나 데이터 접근 비용이 비싸서 상세한 지도가 없어요.

sentences.text: Volunteer platforms like OpenStreetMap let people worldwide create and share free digital maps.
sentences.translation: OpenStreetMap 같은 자원봉사 플랫폼을 통해 전 세계 사람들이 무료 디지털 지도를 만들고 공유할 수 있어요.

sentences.text: This approach proved effective during the 2010 Haiti earthquake, when volunteers mapped Port-au-Prince in just days.
sentences.translation: 이 방식은 2010년 아이티 지진 때 자원봉사자들이 며칠 만에 포르토프랭스를 지도화하면서 효과가 입증되었어요.

sentences.text: Open data allows any aid group to use the maps instantly without cost, which saves critical time.
sentences.translation: 개방 데이터 덕분에 어떤 구호 단체든 비용 없이 즉시 지도를 사용할 수 있고, 이것이 결정적인 시간을 절약해 줘요.

sentences.text: The writer argues that sharing geographic information freely is not just convenient but a matter of public safety.
sentences.translation: 글쓴이는 지리 정보를 무료로 공유하는 것이 단순한 편의가 아니라 공공 안전의 문제라고 주장해요.

sentences.text: This expository article uses a problem-solution structure to argue for open-access geographic data in disaster response.
sentences.translation: 이 설명문은 문제-해결 구조를 사용하여 재난 대응에서의 개방형 지리 데이터를 주장해요.

sentences.text: The writer establishes urgency by opening with a concrete disaster scenario and immediately linking it to map availability.
sentences.translation: 글쓴이는 구체적인 재난 시나리오로 시작하여 즉시 지도 가용성과 연결함으로써 긴급성을 확립해요.

sentences.text: Passive constructions like "are designed to show" and "is locked behind" emphasize systems and processes rather than individual actors.
sentences.translation: "are designed to show"나 "is locked behind" 같은 수동태 구문이 개별 행위자보다 시스템과 과정을 강조해요.

sentences.text: The Haiti earthquake example serves as a pivotal case study, grounding the abstract argument in a well-known real event.
sentences.translation: 아이티 지진 사례가 핵심 사례 연구로 작용하여, 추상적 주장을 잘 알려진 실제 사건에 근거 지어요.

sentences.text: Causal connectors — "because," "without," "when" — thread through the article, reinforcing the cause-and-effect logic of why open maps matter.
sentences.translation: "because," "without," "when" 같은 인과 연결어가 글 전체를 관통하며, 공개 지도가 왜 중요한지의 인과 논리를 강화해요.

sentences.text: The closing reframes a technical topic as a moral imperative, elevating the argument from practical utility to public duty.
sentences.translation: 마무리에서 기술적 주제를 도덕적 의무로 재구성하여, 주장을 실용적 효용에서 공적 책무로 격상시켜요.

sentences.original: When a disaster strikes — a flood, an earthquake, or a large fire — one of the first things emergency teams need is an accurate, up-to-date map of the affected area.
sentences.translation: 재난이 닥칠 때 — 홍수, 지진, 대형 화재 — 긴급 대응팀이 가장 먼저 필요로 하는 것 중 하나는 피해 지역의 정확하고 최신인 지도이다.

grammarElements.text: a flood, an earthquake, or a large fire
grammarElements.label: 대시 삽입 동격어구

grammarElements.text: one of the first things emergency teams need
grammarElements.label: 주어 (복합 명사구 + 관계절)

grammarElements.text: an accurate, up-to-date map of the affected area
grammarElements.label: 보어 (명사구)

vocabulary.example: The disaster destroyed hundreds of homes.
An earthquake can strike without warning.
We need accurate data to make decisions.
Keep your software up-to-date.

expressions.meaning: 가장 먼저 필요한 것 중 하나
expressions.usage: 우선순위가 높은 사항을 소개할 때 쓰는 표현
재난이나 사건의 영향을 받은 지역을 가리키는 공식적 표현

patterns.pattern: When [event] strikes — [examples] — one of the first things [people] need is [noun]
patterns.explanation: When절로 긴급 상황을 설정하고, 대시 사이에 구체적 예시를 삽입한 뒤, 가장 시급한 필요를 제시하는 도입 패턴.
examples: When a pandemic strikes — a new virus, a mutation, or a resurgence — one of the first things hospitals need is reliable testing equipment.
When a power outage strikes — a storm, a grid failure, or a cyberattack — one of the first things residents need is a charged communication device.

drillQuestions.question: When a disaster _____, emergency teams need maps.
They need an accurate, _____ map of the affected area.
"The affected area" means the area that has been _____.
The dashes in this sentence are used to insert _____.

sentences.notes: 글의 도입부로, When절로 긴급 상황을 설정하고 대시(—)로 구체적 재난 유형을 삽입한다. "one of the first things ~ need is ~" 구조가 지도의 우선순위를 명확히 한다.

koreanAlignment.en: When a disaster strikes
a flood, an earthquake, or a large fire
one of the first things emergency teams need
koreanAlignment.ko: 긴급 대응팀이 가장 먼저 필요로 하는 것 중 하나는

koreanAlignment.en: is an accurate, up-to-date map
of the affected area

pronunciationNotes: disaster: /dɪˈzæstər/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "디재스터"에 가깝다. s가 /z/로 발음된다.
accurate: /ˈækjərət/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "애큐럿"에 가깝다. -ate가 /ət/으로 약하게 발음된다.

sentences.original: Without reliable maps, rescue workers cannot identify which roads are still passable, where shelters have been set up, or how many people live in the zones most at risk.
sentences.translation: 신뢰할 수 있는 지도가 없으면, 구조대원은 어떤 도로가 아직 통행 가능한지, 대피소가 어디에 설치되었는지, 또는 가장 위험한 지역에 얼마나 많은 사람이 사는지 파악할 수 없다.

grammarElements.text: where shelters have been set up
grammarElements.label: 간접의문절 2 (where)

grammarElements.text: or how many people live in the zones most at risk
grammarElements.label: 간접의문절 3 (how many)

vocabulary.example: We need reliable information during a crisis.
Rescue teams arrived within hours.
Only two roads remained passable after the flood.
Emergency shelters were opened for residents.

expressions.usage: Without + 명사로 부재 상태를 조건처럼 나타내는 표현
위험도가 가장 높은 대상이나 지역을 가리킬 때 쓴다

patterns.pattern: Without [resource], [people] cannot [verb] which [A], where [B], or how [C]
patterns.explanation: 핵심 자원의 부재가 여러 중요한 판단을 불가능하게 만든다는 것을 세 가지 간접의문절로 나열하는 패턴.
examples: Without proper data, managers cannot determine which departments are understaffed, where costs are rising, or how productivity compares to last year.
Without internet access, students cannot check which assignments are due, where resources are posted, or how to contact their teachers.

drillQuestions.question: _____ reliable maps, rescue workers cannot identify safe roads.
Rescue workers cannot identify which roads are still _____.
"Where shelters have been set up" is an indirect _____ clause.
People in the zones most at _____ need help first.

sentences.notes: Without로 시작해 지도 부재의 결과를 강조한다. 세 개의 간접의문절(which, where, how many)을 병렬로 나열해 지도가 제공하는 정보의 다양성을 보여준다. 수동태(have been set up)가 대피소 설치의 결과 상태를 나타낸다.

koreanAlignment.en: Without reliable maps
rescue workers cannot identify
which roads are still passable
where shelters have been set up
or how many people live in the zones most at risk
koreanAlignment.ko: 가장 위험한 지역에 얼마나 많은 사람이 사는지

pronunciationNotes: reliable: /rɪˈlaɪəbl/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "릴라이어블"에 가깝다.
shelters: /ˈʃeltərz/ — 첫 음절에 강세. sh를 "쉬"처럼 발음. "쉘터즈"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: In many parts of the world, detailed maps simply do not exist, because the areas were never formally surveyed or because the data is locked behind expensive licenses.
sentences.translation: 세계의 많은 지역에서 상세한 지도는 단순히 존재하지 않는데, 그 지역이 한 번도 공식적으로 측량된 적이 없거나 데이터가 비싼 라이선스 뒤에 잠겨 있기 때문이다.

grammarElements.text: In many parts of the world
grammarElements.label: 장소 전치사구

grammarElements.text: because the areas were never formally surveyed
grammarElements.label: 이유절 1 (because + 수동태)

grammarElements.text: or because the data is locked behind expensive licenses
grammarElements.label: 이유절 2 (because + 수동태)

vocabulary.example: We need a more detailed map of this area.
The region was never formally mapped.
The land was surveyed before construction began.
The data requires an expensive license to access.

expressions.usage: simply를 넣어 사실의 명백함을 강조하는 표현
접근이 차단되어 있음을 은유적으로 표현할 때 쓴다

patterns.pattern: [Noun] simply do not exist, because [reason A] or because [reason B]
patterns.explanation: 어떤 자원이 존재하지 않음을 단정한 뒤, 두 가지 이유를 because절로 병렬 제시하는 패턴.
examples: Affordable options simply do not exist, because demand far exceeds supply or because regulations block new providers.
Reliable records simply do not exist, because the systems were never digitized or because the files were destroyed in the war.

drillQuestions.question: Detailed maps simply do not _____ in many parts of the world.
The areas were never formally _____.
"Locked behind expensive licenses" means the data is not freely _____.
The sentence gives _____ reasons why maps do not exist.

sentences.notes: "simply do not exist"로 현실의 심각성을 강조한다. 두 개의 because절을 or로 연결해 지도 부재의 이유를 설명하며, 수동태(were never surveyed, is locked)가 체계적 결함을 부각한다.

koreanAlignment.en: In many parts of the world
detailed maps simply do not exist
because the areas were never formally surveyed
koreanAlignment.ko: 그 지역이 공식적으로 측량된 적이 없거나

koreanAlignment.en: or because the data is locked behind expensive licenses
koreanAlignment.ko: 데이터가 비싼 라이선스 뒤에 잠겨 있기 때문이다

pronunciationNotes: surveyed: /sərˈveɪd/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "서베이드"에 가깝다.
licenses: /ˈlaɪsənsɪz/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "라이선시즈"에 가깝다. 끝의 -ses를 /sɪz/로 발음한다.

sentences.original: This is where public mapping projects have made an enormous difference.
sentences.translation: 바로 이 지점에서 공공 지도 제작 프로젝트가 엄청난 변화를 만들어 냈다.

grammarElements.text: where public mapping projects have made an enormous difference
grammarElements.label: 보어 (where 명사절)

vocabulary.example: Public data should be accessible to everyone.
Digital mapping has transformed disaster response.
Volunteers have made an enormous contribution.

expressions.usage: 앞선 문제에 대한 해결책이나 전환점을 도입할 때 쓰는 표현
큰 영향이나 기여를 했음을 표현할 때 쓴다

patterns.pattern: This is where [subject] have made [adjective] difference
patterns.explanation: 앞서 제기된 문제에 대해 해결의 전환점을 선언하는 짧고 강력한 패턴.
examples: This is where community organizations have made a real difference.
This is where technology has made a significant difference.

drillQuestions.question: This is _____ public mapping projects have made a difference.
Public mapping projects have made an _____ difference.
"This is where" introduces a _____ to the problem.
"Have made" is in the present _____ tense.

sentences.notes: 짧지만 글의 논리 전환을 이끄는 핵심 문장이다. "This is where ~"로 앞의 문제 진단에서 해결책 제시로 자연스럽게 넘어간다. 현재완료(have made)가 지속적 성과를 나타낸다.

koreanAlignment.en: This is where
public mapping projects
have made an enormous difference

pronunciationNotes: enormous: /ɪˈnɔːrməs/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "이노머스"에 가깝다.
mapping: /ˈmæpɪŋ/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "매핑"에 가깝다. p를 확실히 발음한다.

sentences.original: Platforms like OpenStreetMap allow volunteers around the world to add roads, buildings, and landmarks to a shared digital map that anyone can use for free.
sentences.translation: OpenStreetMap 같은 플랫폼은 전 세계 자원봉사자들이 도로, 건물, 랜드마크를 누구나 무료로 사용할 수 있는 공유 디지털 지도에 추가할 수 있게 해 준다.

grammarElements.text: to add roads, buildings, and landmarks
grammarElements.label: to 부정사 (목적보어)

grammarElements.text: to a shared digital map that anyone can use for free
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 + 관계절

vocabulary.example: Many platforms offer open-source tools.
Thousands of volunteers contributed their time.
The map includes all major landmarks.
The document is on a shared drive.

expressions.usage: 주어가 목적어에게 어떤 행위를 가능하게 하는 구조
비용 없이 이용할 수 있음을 나타내는 일상 표현

patterns.pattern: [Platform] allow(s) [people] to [verb A], [verb B], and [verb C] to [shared resource] that anyone can [verb] for free
patterns.explanation: 플랫폼이 사용자에게 여러 행위를 가능하게 하고, 그 결과물이 모두에게 개방됨을 설명하는 패턴.
examples: Wikimedia allows editors around the world to write, edit, and translate articles on a shared encyclopedia that anyone can read for free.
GitHub allows developers to create, share, and review code on a shared platform that anyone can access for free.

drillQuestions.question: Platforms like OpenStreetMap _____ volunteers to add data.
Volunteers add roads, buildings, and _____ to the map.
"That anyone can use for free" is a _____ clause describing the map.
"A shared digital map" means a map that is _____ by many people.

sentences.notes: "allow + 목적어 + to부정사" 구조로 플랫폼의 기능을 설명한다. 관계절(that anyone can use for free)이 개방성의 핵심을 간결하게 표현한다. 세 가지 요소(roads, buildings, landmarks)의 나열이 기여의 범위를 보여준다.

koreanAlignment.en: Platforms like OpenStreetMap
allow volunteers around the world
to add roads, buildings, and landmarks
koreanAlignment.ko: 도로, 건물, 랜드마크를 추가할 수 있게 해 준다

koreanAlignment.en: to a shared digital map
that anyone can use for free

pronunciationNotes: volunteers: /ˌvɑːlənˈtɪrz/ — 세 번째 음절에 강세. "발런티어즈"에 가깝다.
landmarks: /ˈlændmɑːrks/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "랜드마크스"에 가깝다. d와 m 사이 자연스럽게 이어 발음한다.

sentences.original: During the 2010 earthquake in Haiti, thousands of online volunteers traced satellite images and created a usable street map of Port-au-Prince within days, giving aid organizations a tool that had not existed before the disaster.
sentences.translation: 2010년 아이티 지진 당시, 수천 명의 온라인 자원봉사자가 위성 이미지를 추적하여 며칠 만에 포르토프랭스의 사용 가능한 거리 지도를 만들어 냈고, 재난 이전에는 존재하지 않았던 도구를 구호 단체에 제공했다.

grammarElements.text: and created a usable street map of Port-au-Prince within days
grammarElements.label: 동사구 2 (병렬)

grammarElements.text: giving aid organizations a tool that had not existed before the disaster
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (결과) + 관계절 (과거완료)

vocabulary.example: Volunteers traced outlines of buildings from satellite photos.
Satellite images showed the extent of the damage.
The map became usable within days.
Aid organizations arrived after the earthquake.

expressions.usage: 놀라울 정도로 짧은 기간을 강조할 때 쓰는 표현
과거완료로 재난 이전 시점의 부재를 나타낸다

patterns.pattern: During [event], [people] [verb A] and [verb B] within [time], giving [recipient] [noun] that had not existed before
patterns.explanation: 특정 사건 중에 빠른 행동으로 이전에 없던 것을 만들어 냈음을 서술하는 패턴. 분사구문(giving)이 결과를 자연스럽게 이어 준다.
examples: During the 2020 pandemic, teams of developers designed and launched a contact-tracing app within weeks, giving health authorities a tool that had not existed before.
During the crisis, local farmers harvested and donated supplies within hours, giving shelters food that had not been available before.

drillQuestions.question: Thousands of volunteers _____ satellite images to create maps.
They created a usable map _____ days.
"Giving aid organizations a tool" is a _____ phrase showing the result.
The tool "had not existed" uses the past _____ tense.

sentences.notes: 아이티 사례를 통해 자원봉사 지도 제작의 실효성을 증명하는 핵심 문장이다. 분사구문(giving)이 결과를 자연스럽게 이어 주고, 과거완료(had not existed)가 "이전에는 없었던" 시간 차이를 명확히 한다.

koreanAlignment.en: During the 2010 earthquake in Haiti
thousands of online volunteers
traced satellite images
and created a usable street map of Port-au-Prince within days
koreanAlignment.ko: 며칠 만에 포르토프랭스의 사용 가능한 거리 지도를 만들어 냈고

koreanAlignment.en: giving aid organizations a tool that had not existed before the disaster
koreanAlignment.ko: 재난 이전에는 존재하지 않았던 도구를 구호 단체에 제공했다

pronunciationNotes: satellite: /ˈsætəlaɪt/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "새틀라잇"에 가깝다. 가운데 음절을 약하게 발음한다.
organizations: /ˌɔːrɡənəˈzeɪʃənz/ — 네 번째 음절에 강세. "오거너제이션즈"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: Since then, the same approach has been repeated in dozens of emergencies, from typhoons in the Philippines to flooding in Mozambique.
sentences.translation: 그 이후로, 같은 접근법이 필리핀의 태풍부터 모잠비크의 홍수까지 수십 건의 비상 상황에서 반복되어 왔다.

grammarElements.text: from typhoons in the Philippines to flooding in Mozambique
grammarElements.label: from ~ to ~ (범위 표현)

vocabulary.example: The same approach works in different situations.
The experiment has been repeated many times.
Dozens of volunteers signed up.
Typhoons hit the Philippines every year.

expressions.usage: 특정 시점 이후 지금까지를 나타내는 시간 표현
범위나 다양성을 나타낼 때 쓰는 표현

patterns.pattern: Since then, the same [noun] has been [past participle] in [quantity] of [contexts], from [A] to [B]
patterns.explanation: 특정 사건 이후로 같은 방식이 다양한 맥락에서 반복되었음을 현재완료 수동태로 서술하는 패턴.
examples: Since then, the same model has been adopted in dozens of countries, from small island nations to large continental states.
Since then, the same technique has been applied in hundreds of studies, from laboratory experiments to field research.

drillQuestions.question: _____ then, the same approach has been repeated.
The approach has been repeated in _____ of emergencies.
"Has been repeated" is the present perfect _____ voice.
"From typhoons to flooding" shows the _____ of emergencies.

sentences.notes: 현재완료 수동태(has been repeated)로 아이티 이후 지속적 적용을 강조한다. "from ~ to ~"로 지리적·유형적 다양성을 보여주며 설득력을 높인다.

koreanAlignment.en: Since then
the same approach has been repeated
in dozens of emergencies
from typhoons in the Philippines to flooding in Mozambique

pronunciationNotes: approach: /əˈproʊtʃ/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "어프로치"에 가깝다.
Mozambique: /ˌmoʊzæmˈbiːk/ — 세 번째 음절에 강세. "모잠비크"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: The maps are designed to show not just geography but also critical information: the locations of hospitals, water sources, evacuation routes, and areas where buildings have collapsed.
sentences.translation: 이 지도는 단순히 지리만이 아니라 중요한 정보도 보여 주도록 설계되어 있다: 병원의 위치, 수원지, 대피 경로, 그리고 건물이 붕괴된 지역.

grammarElements.text: not just geography but also critical information
grammarElements.label: 목적어 (not just ~ but also ~)

grammarElements.text: the locations of hospitals, water sources, evacuation routes, and areas where buildings have collapsed
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 동격 나열 + 관계절

vocabulary.example: The system is designed to handle emergencies.
This is critical information for rescue teams.
Evacuation routes must be clearly marked.
Several buildings collapsed during the earthquake.

expressions.usage: 예상보다 더 넓은 범위를 포함함을 강조하는 상관접속사
의도적으로 특정 기능을 갖추고 있음을 나타내는 수동태 표현

patterns.pattern: [Subject] are designed to [verb] not just [A] but also [B]: [list of specifics]
patterns.explanation: 수동태로 설계 의도를 밝힌 뒤, not just ~ but also로 범위를 확장하고 콜론 뒤에 구체적 항목을 나열하는 정보 설계 패턴.
examples: The dashboard is designed to display not just sales data but also customer feedback: ratings, comments, return rates, and complaint categories.
The report is designed to cover not just financial results but also social impact: community programs, employee well-being, environmental efforts, and charitable giving.

drillQuestions.question: The maps are _____ to show critical information.
The maps show not just geography _____ also critical information.
_____ routes help people leave a dangerous area safely.
"Areas where buildings have collapsed" uses a _____ clause.

sentences.notes: 수동태(are designed to show)로 지도의 의도적 설계를 강조한다. "not just ~ but also ~"로 기능의 범위를 확장하고, 콜론 뒤 네 가지 구체적 정보를 나열해 실용성을 보여준다.

koreanAlignment.en: The maps are designed to show
koreanAlignment.ko: 이 지도는 보여 주도록 설계되어 있다

koreanAlignment.en: not just geography but also critical information
the locations of hospitals, water sources, evacuation routes
and areas where buildings have collapsed

pronunciationNotes: evacuation: /ɪˌvækjuˈeɪʃən/ — 네 번째 음절에 강세. "이배큐에이션"에 가깝다.
collapsed: /kəˈlæpst/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "컬랩스트"에 가깝다. -ed가 /t/로 발음된다.

sentences.original: Because the data is open, any relief agency can download it, print it, or load it onto a phone without waiting for permission or paying a fee.
sentences.translation: 데이터가 개방되어 있기 때문에, 어떤 구호 기관이든 허가를 기다리거나 비용을 지불하지 않고 데이터를 다운로드하거나, 인쇄하거나, 휴대전화에 불러올 수 있다.

grammarElements.text: can download it, print it, or load it onto a phone
grammarElements.label: 동사 (병렬 3개)

grammarElements.text: without waiting for permission or paying a fee
grammarElements.label: without + 동명사구 (부재 조건)

vocabulary.example: Relief agencies sent supplies to the disaster zone.
Several agencies coordinated the rescue effort.
You do not need permission to use this data.
There is no fee to access the map.

expressions.usage: without + 동명사로 불필요한 절차 생략을 나타내는 표현
데이터를 기기에 설치하거나 옮기는 것을 나타내는 기술 표현

patterns.pattern: Because [condition], any [entity] can [verb A], [verb B], or [verb C] without [barrier A] or [barrier B]
patterns.explanation: 이유절로 조건을 밝힌 뒤, 세 가지 행동이 가능하며 장벽이 없음을 강조하는 개방성 설명 패턴.
examples: Because the software is open-source, any developer can modify it, distribute it, or build upon it without buying a license or signing an agreement.
Because the curriculum is free, any school can adopt it, adapt it, or share it without requesting permission or paying royalties.

drillQuestions.question: _____ the data is open, any agency can use it.
Relief agencies can download, print, or _____ the data onto a phone.
"Without waiting for permission" means they do not need to _____.
"Any relief agency" means _____ agency can use the data.

sentences.notes: Because절로 개방 데이터의 이점을 설명한다. 세 가지 병렬 동사(download, print, load)가 접근의 다양성을 보여주고, "without ~ or ~"이 장벽 부재를 강조한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Because the data is open
any relief agency can download it, print it, or load it onto a phone
koreanAlignment.ko: 어떤 구호 기관이든 다운로드하거나, 인쇄하거나, 휴대전화에 불러올 수 있다

koreanAlignment.en: without waiting for permission
or paying a fee

pronunciationNotes: relief: /rɪˈliːf/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "릴리프"에 가깝다. f를 확실히 발음한다.
permission: /pərˈmɪʃən/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "퍼미션"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: Speed matters in emergencies — every hour spent searching for basic spatial information is an hour that could have been used to deliver supplies or evacuate residents.
sentences.translation: 비상 상황에서는 속도가 중요하다 — 기본적인 공간 정보를 찾는 데 쓰인 매 시간은 물자를 전달하거나 주민을 대피시키는 데 쓰일 수 있었던 시간이다.

grammarElements.text: every hour spent searching for basic spatial information
grammarElements.label: 주어 (과거분사 수식)

grammarElements.text: an hour that could have been used to deliver supplies or evacuate residents
grammarElements.label: 보어 (관계절 + could have been)

vocabulary.example: Spatial data helps us understand where things are.
Trucks delivered supplies to the shelter.
Authorities evacuated the coastal town before the storm.
Emergency supplies were sent by helicopter.

expressions.usage: 시간의 긴급성을 단도직입적으로 표현할 때 쓴다
실현되지 않은 가능성을 나타내는 가정법 과거완료 수동태

patterns.pattern: [Noun] matters in [context] — every [time unit] spent [verb-ing] is [time unit] that could have been used to [verb]
patterns.explanation: 핵심 가치를 선언한 뒤, 대시 이후 낭비되는 시간과 그 기회비용을 대비시키는 긴급성 강조 패턴.
examples: Accuracy matters in medicine — every minute spent correcting errors is a minute that could have been used to treat patients.
Efficiency matters in manufacturing — every hour spent on rework is an hour that could have been used to produce new goods.

drillQuestions.question: Speed _____ in emergencies.
Every hour spent searching for information _____ have been used to help people.
"Spatial information" refers to information about _____.
"Evacuate residents" means to _____ people from a dangerous area.

sentences.notes: "Speed matters"라는 짧은 선언 뒤, 대시로 기회비용을 구체화한다. "could have been used"(가정법 과거완료 수동태)가 시간 낭비의 대가를 실감하게 한다. 논증에서 긴급성을 호소하는 강력한 수사법이다.

koreanAlignment.en: Speed matters in emergencies
every hour spent searching for basic spatial information
koreanAlignment.ko: 기본적인 공간 정보를 찾는 데 쓰인 매 시간은

koreanAlignment.en: is an hour that could have been used
to deliver supplies or evacuate residents

pronunciationNotes: spatial: /ˈspeɪʃəl/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "스페이셜"에 가깝다. -tial이 /ʃəl/로 발음된다.
evacuate: /ɪˈvækjueɪt/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "이배큐에잇"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: Public maps also help communities prepare before a disaster happens, because residents can see which neighborhoods lie in flood zones and where the nearest safe areas are located.
sentences.translation: 공공 지도는 재난이 발생하기 전에 지역사회가 대비하는 데도 도움이 되는데, 주민들이 어떤 동네가 홍수 구역에 있는지, 가장 가까운 안전 지역이 어디에 있는지 확인할 수 있기 때문이다.

grammarElements.text: because residents can see which neighborhoods lie in flood zones and where the nearest safe areas are located
grammarElements.label: 이유절 (because) + 간접의문절 2개

vocabulary.example: Communities work together to prepare for emergencies.
Some neighborhoods are more at risk than others.
Houses in flood zones need special insurance.
The shelter is located near the school.

expressions.usage: help + 목적어 + 원형부정사로 도움의 내용을 나타내는 구조
예방적 조치의 시점을 나타내는 시간절

patterns.pattern: [Subject] also help [people] [verb] before [event], because [people] can see which [A] and where [B]
patterns.explanation: 추가적 이점을 also로 도입하고, because절로 그 이유를 두 가지 간접의문절로 구체화하는 패턴.
examples: Weather apps also help farmers plan before a storm arrives, because they can see which fields are at risk and where the heaviest rain will fall.
Safety audits also help schools improve before an incident occurs, because staff can see which exits are blocked and where equipment is missing.

drillQuestions.question: Public maps also help communities _____ before a disaster.
Residents can see which neighborhoods _____ in flood zones.
"Before a disaster happens" is a _____ clause.
"The nearest safe areas are located" uses the _____ voice.

sentences.notes: also로 지도의 추가적 기능(사전 대비)을 도입한다. because절 안에 두 개의 간접의문절(which ~ and where ~)이 주민이 확인 가능한 정보를 구체적으로 보여준다.

koreanAlignment.en: Public maps also help communities prepare
koreanAlignment.ko: 공공 지도는 지역사회가 대비하는 데도 도움이 되는데

koreanAlignment.en: before a disaster happens
because residents can see
which neighborhoods lie in flood zones
and where the nearest safe areas are located
koreanAlignment.ko: 가장 가까운 안전 지역이 어디에 있는지

pronunciationNotes: neighborhoods: /ˈneɪbərˌhʊdz/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "네이버후즈"에 가깝다. gh는 묵음이다.
located: /loʊˈkeɪtɪd/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "로케이티드"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: Governments that support open mapping initiatives invest relatively little money but gain a tool that can save lives when it is needed most.
sentences.translation: 공개 지도 제작 이니셔티브를 지원하는 정부는 비교적 적은 비용을 투자하지만, 가장 필요한 순간에 생명을 구할 수 있는 도구를 얻는다.

grammarElements.text: Governments that support open mapping initiatives
grammarElements.label: 주어 + 관계절

grammarElements.text: gain a tool that can save lives when it is needed most
grammarElements.label: 동사 2 + 목적어 + 관계절 + 시간절

vocabulary.example: The government launched several new initiatives.
Governments should invest in open data.
The cost is relatively low.
They gain a powerful tool at low cost.

expressions.usage: 투입 비용이 성과에 비해 작음을 나타낼 때 쓴다
긴급한 상황에서의 가치를 강조하는 표현

patterns.pattern: [Entity] that [action] invest(s) relatively little [resource] but gain(s) [outcome] that can [benefit] when [condition]
patterns.explanation: 주체의 행동을 관계절로 특정한 뒤, 적은 투자 대비 큰 효과를 but으로 대비시키는 비용-편익 논증 패턴.
examples: Schools that adopt open-source textbooks invest relatively little effort but gain materials that can be updated whenever the curriculum changes.
Cities that install bike lanes invest relatively little space but gain infrastructure that can reduce traffic when commuting hours peak.

drillQuestions.question: Governments that support mapping initiatives _____ relatively little money.
They gain a tool that can save _____ when needed most.
"Relatively little money" means the cost is _____.
"When it is needed most" refers to times of _____.

sentences.notes: 관계절(that support open mapping initiatives)로 대상을 특정하고, "invest little but gain ~" 구조로 비용-편익을 명확히 대비한다. "when it is needed most"가 긴급 시점의 가치를 강조한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Governments that support open mapping initiatives
koreanAlignment.ko: 공개 지도 제작 이니셔티브를 지원하는 정부는

koreanAlignment.en: invest relatively little money
but gain a tool
that can save lives when it is needed most
koreanAlignment.ko: 가장 필요한 순간에 생명을 구할 수 있는

pronunciationNotes: initiatives: /ɪˈnɪʃətɪvz/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "이니셔티브즈"에 가깝다.
relatively: /ˈrelətɪvli/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "렐러티블리"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: The value of a good map is easy to overlook during ordinary times, yet it becomes instantly obvious the moment a crisis begins.
sentences.translation: 좋은 지도의 가치는 평상시에는 간과하기 쉽지만, 위기가 시작되는 순간 즉각적으로 명백해진다.

grammarElements.text: The value of a good map
grammarElements.label: 주어 (of 전치사구 수식)

vocabulary.example: The value of preparation becomes clear after a disaster.
It is easy to overlook small but important details.
During ordinary times, we forget how important maps are.
The answer became obvious after the test.

expressions.usage: tough 구문(주어 + be + 형용사 + to 부정사)으로 대상의 특성을 표현
접속사처럼 쓰여 "~하자마자"라는 즉시성을 나타낸다

patterns.pattern: [Noun] is easy to [verb] during [normal time], yet it becomes [adverb] [adjective] the moment [event]
patterns.explanation: 평상시의 무관심과 위기 시의 절실함을 yet으로 대비시키는 극적 전환 패턴.
examples: The importance of backup systems is easy to forget during normal operations, yet it becomes painfully clear the moment a server crashes.
The need for insurance is easy to dismiss during healthy years, yet it becomes urgently important the moment an illness strikes.

drillQuestions.question: The value of a good map is easy to _____ during ordinary times.
It becomes instantly _____ when a crisis begins.
"The moment a crisis begins" means _____ a crisis starts.
"Yet" in this sentence signals a _____.

sentences.notes: tough 구문(is easy to overlook)으로 평상시의 간과를 표현하고, yet으로 위기 시의 극적 전환을 보여준다. "the moment"가 접속사처럼 쓰여 즉시성을 강조한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The value of a good map
is easy to overlook
during ordinary times
yet it becomes instantly obvious
the moment a crisis begins

pronunciationNotes: overlook: /ˌoʊvərˈlʊk/ — 세 번째 음절에 강세. "오버룩"에 가깝다.
obvious: /ˈɑːbviəs/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "아브비어스"에 가깝다. b 다음 v를 연속 발음한다.

sentences.original: Sharing geographic knowledge freely is not just a technical convenience — it is a matter of public safety.
sentences.translation: 지리적 지식을 자유롭게 공유하는 것은 단순한 기술적 편의가 아니다 — 그것은 공공 안전의 문제이다.

grammarElements.text: it is a matter of public safety
grammarElements.label: 대시 뒤 재정의 절

vocabulary.example: Geographic data is vital for disaster response.
Online shopping is a modern convenience.
This is a matter of life and death.
Public safety is the government's responsibility.

expressions.usage: 대상의 의미를 격상시킬 때 쓰는 재정의 표현. 대시가 극적 전환을 만든다
어떤 이슈가 안전과 직결됨을 강조할 때 쓰는 격식 표현

patterns.pattern: [Gerund phrase] is not just [lesser description] — it is a matter of [higher value]
patterns.explanation: 동명사 주어로 행위를 명사화한 뒤, not just로 낮은 평가를 부정하고 대시 뒤에 더 높은 가치로 재정의하는 결론 패턴.
examples: Teaching critical thinking is not just an academic exercise — it is a matter of democratic participation.
Protecting clean water is not just an environmental goal — it is a matter of human survival.

drillQuestions.question: Sharing geographic knowledge freely is not just a technical _____.
It is a _____ of public safety.
"Sharing geographic knowledge" is a _____ phrase used as the subject.
The dash in this sentence is used to introduce a stronger _____.

sentences.notes: 글 전체의 결론이자 핵심 주장. 동명사 주어(Sharing geographic knowledge freely)로 행위를 명사화하고, "not just ~ — it is ~" 구조로 기술적 편의에서 공공 안전이라는 더 높은 가치로 격상시킨다. 의견문의 마무리로 강한 인상을 남긴다.

koreanAlignment.en: Sharing geographic knowledge freely
is not just a technical convenience
it is a matter of public safety

pronunciationNotes: geographic: /ˌdʒiːəˈɡræfɪk/ — 세 번째 음절에 강세. "지어그래픽"에 가깝다.
convenience: /kənˈviːniəns/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "컨비니언스"에 가깝다.
